div1.entryFree: Claw, vb., to scratch in a pleasing manner: look, whether the withered elder hath not his poll --ed like a parrot, H4B II, 4, 282. if a talent be a claw, look how he --s him with a talent, LLL IV, 2, 66. Figuratively, == to smooth, to humour: laugh when I am merry and c. no man in his humour, Ado I, 3, 18. -- Used blunderingly: but age hath --ed me in his clutch, Hml. V, 1, 80 (Ff caught).
